title: Right to Information Act 2009
description: The Right to Information Act 2009 guarantees every citizen of Bangladesh the right to access information held by public authorities, establishing Bangladesh as one of the first South Asian countries to enact comprehensive freedom of information legislation. The Act establishes the Information Commission as the independent oversight body and creates obligations for public authorities to disclose information proactively and to respond to individual requests.

provisions.title: Section 1. Short Title, Extent and Commencement
provisions.content: This Act may be called the Right to Information Act, 2009. It extends to the whole of Bangladesh. It shall come into force on such date as the Government may, by notification in the official Gazette, appoint and different dates may be appointed for different provisions of this Act. The Act recognizes the right to information as a fundamental right guaranteed by the Constitution of Bangladesh. The purpose of the Act is to ensure transparency and accountability in all public authorities through an effective and efficient mechanism for providing access to information. The Act shall apply to public authorities as defined herein, and shall be interpreted in a manner that gives effect to the constitutional right to information and promotes transparency and accountability in governance.

provisions.title: Section 2. Definitions
provisions.content: In this Act: 'information' means any material in any form, including records, documents, memos, e-mails, opinions, advices, press releases, circulars, orders, logbooks, contracts, reports, papers, samples, models, data material held in any electronic form and information relating to any private body which can be accessed by a public authority under any other law for the time being in force; 'public authority' means any authority, body or institution of self-governance established or constituted by or under the Constitution, or by any other law made by Parliament, or by any order, decision or notification issued or made by the Government; 'citizen' means a citizen of Bangladesh, and for the purposes of access to information does not include foreign nationals or corporations unless otherwise specified; 'Information Commission' means the Information Commission established under this Act; 'Designated Officer' means the officer designated by a public authority to receive and process requests for information.

provisions.title: Section 4. Right to Information
provisions.content: Every citizen shall have the right to obtain any information from the authority that holds the information. The authority shall be bound to provide the information within the stipulated time frame. Every citizen shall be entitled to — (a) inspect works, documents, records; (b) take notes, extracts or certified copies of documents or records; (c) take certified samples of material; (d) obtain information in the form of diskettes, floppies, tapes, video cassettes or in any other electronic mode or through printouts where such information is stored in a computer or in any other device. The right to information may be exercised without requiring the citizen to state any reason for the request or provide any personal information other than that which is necessary to contact the citizen with the requested information. The right to information extends to information in the possession or control of public authorities, including information generated, obtained, or financed with public resources.

provisions.title: Section 7. Obligations of Public Authorities — Proactive Disclosure
provisions.content: Every public authority shall publish the following information including through public notice boards, widely circulated newspapers, official websites and wherever possible electronically: (a) the particulars of the organization, functions and duties; (b) the powers and duties of its officers and employees; (c) the procedure followed in the decision making process, including channels of supervision and accountability; (d) the norms set by it for the discharge of its functions; (e) the rules, regulations, instructions, manuals and records, held by it or under its control or used by its employees for discharging its functions; (f) a statement of the categories of documents that are held by it or under its control; (g) the particulars of any arrangement that exists for consultation with, or representation by the members of the public in relation to the formulation of its policy or implementation thereof; (h) a statement of the boards, councils, committees and other bodies consisting of two or more persons constituted as its part or for the purpose of its advice, and as to whether meetings of those boards, councils, committees and other bodies are open to the public or the minutes of such meetings are accessible to the public.

provisions.title: Section 9. Provision of Information
provisions.content: Every public authority shall designate an officer as the Designated Officer to deal with requests from citizens seeking information under the provisions of this Act. The Designated Officer shall on receiving a request for information from a citizen — (a) provide the information in the form in which it is sought unless it would disproportionately divert the resources of the public authority or would be detrimental to the safety or preservation of the record in question; (b) provide the information in any other form that is reasonably accessible; (c) communicate to the citizen in writing the date by which the information requested would be provided. Where access to the record of a public authority is sought, the Designated Officer of the authority shall provide the citizen with access to the record or copy thereof within twenty working days of receipt of the request. Where access to the information concerns the life or liberty of a person, the information shall be provided within 48 hours of receipt of the request.

provisions.title: Section 16. Exempted Information
provisions.content: Notwithstanding anything contained in this Act, there shall be no obligation to give any citizen information which: (a) is connected with foreign states and is likely to harm national security or foreign relations; (b) would harm the implementation of any law of Bangladesh; (c) would reveal private information of individuals, except in the public interest; (d) would adversely affect the interests of any protected species; (e) is held in any investigation; (f) would harm the rights of any third party or their intellectual property rights; (g) would cause economic harm or prejudice any person's right to a fair trial; (h) would harm the personal security of any person; (i) is about cabinet papers and discussions of the cabinet, including records of deliberations of the Council of Ministers, Secretaries and other officers. The exemptions shall be applied proportionately and only to the extent that the disclosure would actually harm the protected interest. The public authority shall segregate exempt material from non-exempt material and provide access to the non-exempt portions.

provisions.title: Section 21. Establishment of Information Commission
provisions.content: The Government shall, for the purposes of this Act, establish by notification in the official Gazette, the Information Commission consisting of a Chief Information Commissioner and such number of Information Commissioners as the Government may think fit. The Chief Information Commissioner and the Information Commissioners shall be appointed by the President of Bangladesh. They shall serve for a period of three years and shall be eligible for reappointment for one additional term. The Information Commission shall be independent in the exercise of its functions and its decisions shall be binding on all public authorities. The Commission shall have the following powers: (a) receive and adjudicate appeals; (b) examine records and documents of public authorities; (c) require public authorities to provide information in specified forms; (d) impose penalties for non-compliance; (e) recommend disciplinary action against officials who violate the Act.

provisions.title: Section 27. Penalty
provisions.content: Where the Information Commission is satisfied that any Designated Officer has, without any reasonable cause, refused to receive an application for information, or has not furnished information within the time specified under this Act, or has malafidely denied the request for information, or knowingly given incorrect, incomplete or misleading information, or destroyed information which was the subject of the request, or obstructed in any manner the furnishing of information — it shall impose a penalty of five hundred taka for each day of delay from the date of expiry of the time limit specified for providing information. The total amount of such penalty shall not exceed twenty five thousand taka. The officer responsible for non-compliance shall also be subject to disciplinary proceedings under the relevant service rules. Where non-compliance is persistent or deliberate, the Commission may recommend criminal prosecution. The Commission shall publicize cases of non-compliance as a deterrent.

provisions.title: Section 28. Annual Report
provisions.content: The Information Commission shall prepare a report on the implementation of the provisions of this Act in the previous year and forward a copy thereof to the appropriate Government. Each report so prepared shall state the number of requests made to each public authority; the number of decisions where requests for information were not complied with and the provisions of this Act under which these decisions were made; the number of appeals referred to the Information Commission; the number of cases in which the requester was not satisfied with the response given by a public authority; and the number of cases in which the Information Commission imposed penalties and the amount of penalties imposed. The Annual Report shall be laid before Parliament. The Government shall use the Annual Report to evaluate the implementation of this Act and to take measures to improve access to information.

definitions.definition: Any material in any form, including records, documents, memos, e-mails, opinions, advices, press releases, circulars, orders, logbooks, contracts, reports, papers, samples, models, and data material held in any electronic form.
Any authority, body or institution of self-governance established by or under the Constitution, or by any law made by Parliament, or by any order, decision or notification issued by the Government.
The officer designated by a public authority to receive and process requests for information under the Right to Information Act 2009.
The independent body established under the Right to Information Act 2009 to adjudicate appeals against refusals of information requests and to oversee compliance by public authorities.
